zh.Whorlton Castle, Whorlton, Yorkshire, En: 英國英格蘭越可爾爾頓
zh.Cleveland, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾克利夫蘭
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Ros於1301年之前出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，她的父親Sir William de Ros II46歲，母親Maud de Vaux40歲。她於1322年8月與Nicholas de Meynell在英國英格蘭越可爾爾頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1344年7月4日之前。

zh-hans.Whorlton Castle, Whorlton, Yorkshire, En: 英国英格兰越可爾爾頓
zh-hans.Cleveland, Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾克利夫蘭
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Ros于1301年之前出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，她的父亲Sir William de Ros II46岁，母亲Maud de Vaux40岁。她于1322年8月与Nicholas de Meynell在英国英格兰越可爾爾頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1344年7月4日之前。

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Ros was born before 1301, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William de Ros II, was 46 and her mother, Maud de Vaux, was 40. She married Nicholas de Meynell in August 1322, in Whorlton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died before 4 July 1344.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Ros est née avant 1301, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ros II, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Maud de Vaux, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas de Meynell en août 1322, à Whorlton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 4 juillet 1344.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Ros vor 1301 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ros II 46 und ihre Mutter Maud de Vaux 40. Sie hat im August 1322 in Whorlton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Nicholas de Meynell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 4. Juli 1344.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Ros è nata il prima del 1301 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de Ros II, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Maud de Vaux, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Nicholas de Meynell nel agosto 1322, nel Whorlton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 4 luglio 1344.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Rosが1301年以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ros IIは46歳，母Maud de Vauxは40歳であった。1322年8月にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルルトンでNicholas de Meynellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1344年7月4日以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Ros(이)가 1301년 이전 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ros II(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Vaux(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1322년 8월 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 화를턴에서 Nicholas de Meynell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1344년 7월 4일 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Ros nasceu antes 1301, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ros II, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Vaux, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Nicholas de Meynell em agosto de 1322, em Whorlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 4 de julho de 1344.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Ros родилась перед 1301 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ros II было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Vaux, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas de Meynell август 1322 г. в местности уаурлтон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 4 июля 1344 г.

Se casó con Nicholas de Meynell en agosto de 1322, en Whorlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 4 de julio de 1344.
